blurb:
This is where we go when we want Mexican food that doesn't taste like every other place in town. The atmosphere is lively without being too loud, and the service actually knows what they're talking about when you ask questions. Perfect for dinner with friends or when you want something more interesting than Tex-Mex.

Start with the Esquite because it's basically Mexican street corn in a cup and it's addictive. Their tacos are small but packed with flavor, so order several different kinds. The cocktail pitchers are strong and perfect for sharing. Everyone raves about the Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit ones.

They're open late most nights, which is great when you get a craving after 10pm. Gets pretty busy on weekends but the turnover is quick.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is my new favorite spot for girls' night out or any time you want to try something different. The space feels upscale but not stuffy, and the service makes you feel like you're the only table they care about. Perfect when you want to impress out-of-town visitors or celebrate something special.

Start with the hummus and wings, which sounds weird but trust me on this one. The chicken shish kabobs are what I always end up ordering, and they come with this amazing rice that has lentils and raisins mixed in. Their cocktails are creative and strong, and save room for the tiramisu because it's worth the calories.

They close between lunch and dinner, so don't show up at 3pm. Make a reservation for dinner because word is definitely out about this place.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is our go-to when we're craving Indian food that actually tastes like it's made by someone's grandmother. It's in a strip mall by Perimeter Center, nothing fancy about the space, but the food is legit. Perfect for when you want comfort food that happens to be spicy and delicious.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's the real deal. Huge portions that easily feed two people, and they don't skimp on the meat or spices. My husband always gets the chicken biryani and I usually go for one of their curry dishes. Everything comes family-style so you can share and try different things.

Just know they close between lunch and dinner most days, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. Good for takeout if you're feeding a crowd since the portions are massive.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This is perfect when your group can't agree on what to eat because there are twenty different food vendors all in one place. It's right on the square, so you can walk around downtown after eating. Great for families because everyone can get exactly what they want and still sit together.

I usually end up at one of the sandwich places or get tacos, but there's everything from Asian to barbecue. The kids always want whatever looks most interesting that day. It's also good for lunch meetings because it's not too loud and there are plenty of tables.

Parking can be tricky during events on the square, but there are several lots within walking distance. It gets busy on weekends, so plan accordingly.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is the place you go when you want breakfast at 2am or need comfort food after a long day. It's got that classic diner vibe with neon signs and booths, and the fact that it never closes means it's saved me more than once when everything else was shut down. Perfect for late night cravings, early morning meetings, or when the kids want pancakes at weird hours.

The menu is absolutely massive, but the big breakfasts are what they're known for. I always get the Greek omelet because they do it right, and my husband swears by their pancakes. The portions are huge, so come hungry or plan on taking half home.

Just know the parking lot can get crazy during weekend breakfast rush, but it's worth the wait if you need a reliable spot that's always open.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is hands down the best French bakery around here. It feels like stepping into a little cafe in Paris, and the croissants are the real deal - buttery and flaky like they should be. Perfect for weekend brunch or grabbing pastries for a work meeting. The staff actually speaks French, which always makes me smile.

The quiche is what I get for lunch, and their baguettes are so good I usually buy an extra one to take home. The petit fours are beautiful if you need something for a special occasion. Everything tastes like it was made that morning, because it was.

They close at 6pm most days and 3:30pm on Sundays, so don't show up expecting dinner. But for breakfast, lunch, or just really good coffee and pastry, this place is worth the trip.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM

blurb:
If you've never done Brazilian all-you-can-eat barbecue, this is a fun place to try it. They bring different cuts of meat right to your table and you just keep eating until you can't anymore. It's great for celebrating something or when you want to make dinner an event. The whole family can find something they like here.

The cheese bread comes out warm and is addictive, so pace yourself. The feijoada stew is really good if you want to try something traditional Brazilian. They also do breakfast, which is unusual and worth trying on weekends.

It's in the Powers Ferry shopping area, so there's plenty of parking and other shops if you need to walk off dinner afterward.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
I was skeptical about another pizza place, but this one actually lives up to the hype. It's Neapolitan-style with those thin, charred crusts that somehow manage to be crispy and chewy at the same time. Great for date night or when you want pizza that doesn't make you feel guilty afterward.

The Margherita is perfect if you want to taste what they're really about. Simple ingredients but everything tastes like it came straight from Italy. My kids love the Marinara, and I usually go for something with their house-made sausage. They use local ingredients when they can, which you can actually taste.

It gets busy on weekends, so either make a reservation or be prepared to wait. The space is pretty small but cozy.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This little strip mall spot is where I go when I want really good Middle Eastern food without the fancy restaurant prices. It's casual and friendly, perfect for a quick lunch or picking up dinner on the way home. The staff is always nice about explaining dishes if you're not familiar with the menu.

The shawarma is what keeps me coming back, and their falafel is crispy outside and fluffy inside like it should be. They've got good vegetarian options too if that's your thing. Everything feels fresh and made to order, not like it's been sitting under heat lamps.

It's in the Franklin Gateway shopping center, so parking is easy and you can grab groceries next door if you need to.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is where we take my parents when they visit or celebrate anniversaries. Right on the Chattahoochee River with big windows and a deck, so you're basically dining over the water. It's pricey but worth it for special occasions when you want to feel fancy without driving downtown.

The seafood is what they do best. I always get something with their famous crab cakes, and my wife loves their salmon. The Sunday brunch is really popular too, especially if you can get a table by the windows. Service is the kind where they remember your name and refill your water before you ask.

Make a reservation, especially for dinner or weekend brunch. Parking can be tricky during peak times since it's right off Powers Ferry Road.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
